zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch於1220年之前出生在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘時，他的父親Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp28歲，母親Juliana le Brett of Dourton24歲。他於1245年與Alice de Mohun在英國薩默塞特登斯特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1263年10月25日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國薩默塞特斯托克 素布 哈姆頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch was born before 1220, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, his father, Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp, was 28 and his mother, Juliana le Brett of Dourton, was 24. He married Alice de Mohun in 1245, in Dunster, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died before 25 October 1263, in his hometown, and was buried in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch est né avant 1220, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Juliana le Brett of Dourton, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Mohun en 1245, à Dunster, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé avant 25 octobre 1263, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch vor 1220 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp 28 und seine Mutter Juliana le Brett of Dourton 24. Er hat 1245 in Dunster, Somerset, England Alice de Mohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb vor 25. ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatchが1220年以前にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップで生まれた時，父Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchampは28歳，母Juliana le Brett of Dourtonは24歳であった。1245年にイギリスソメルセトダンスターでAlice de Mohunと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1263年10月25日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスソメルセトストク スブ ハムドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch(이)가 1220년 이전 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Juliana le Brett of Dourton(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1245년 영국 서머세트 던스터에서 Alice de Mohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1263년 10월 25일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 스토크 수브 함던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch nasceu antes 1220, em Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Juliana le Brett of Dourton, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Mohun em 1245, em Dunster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Beauchamp Lord of Hatch родился перед 1220 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert IV Beauchamp Lord of Hatch Beauchamp было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Juliana le Brett of Dourton, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Mohun в 1245 г. в местности дунстир, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер перед 25 октябрь 1263 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности сток суб хамдон, сомирсет, Англия.
